NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (TRANSACTION ID # 76723556) FILED BY PLAINTIFF WILLIAMS, JAMES WILLIAMS, DORIS HEARING SET FOR AUG-19-2025 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 304
JOINDER TO DEFENDANT J-M MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC.'S OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE (TRANSACTION ID # 76834611) FILED BY DEFENDANT ROSENDIN ELECTRIC, INC.

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 304, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. 36(A). OPPOSITION FILED BY DEFENDANT J-M MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC. REPLY FILED. THE TRIAL DATE IS DECEMBER 8, 2025, AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(F) IS DECEMBER 17, 2025. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS DECEMBER 2, 2025. TIME TO RESPOND TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY NOT YET SERVED IS SHORTENED TO 20 DAYS. FOR WRITTEN DISCOVERY THAT HAS ALREADY BEEN SERVED, RESPONSES ARE DUE WITHIN 20 DAYS OF THIS HEARING OR BY THE DATE DETERMINED BY THE C.C.P., WHICHEVER IS EARLIER. ELECTRONIC SERVICE IS CONSIDERED THE EQUIVALENT OF PERSONAL SERVICE. THE FACT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS NOVEMBER 21, 2025. THE EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS DECEMBER 5, 2025. A FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR OCTOBER 21, 2025, AT 4:00 P.M. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE STATEMENTS ARE NOT REQUIRED. PARTIES MAY APPEAR AT THE CONFERENCE BY ZOOM. IF THE PARTIES AGREE THAT NO CMC IS NECESSARY, THEY SHALL NOTIFY THE COURT AT LEAST TWO COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, DECEMBER 4, 2025, AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. PERSONAL APPEARANCE IS REQUIRED BY ALL UNLESS A PARTY IS EXCUSED FOR GOOD CAUSE. A REQUEST TO EXCUSE A PERSONAL APPEARANCE MUST BE SUBMITTED TO DEPARTMENT503@SFTC.ORG TWO WEEKS BEFORE THE MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN. CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY. REPORTER: NOT REPORTED (304/EPS).

PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF - FOR TENTATIVE RULING PURPOSES ONLY
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: (mini-minutes 2 of 2)

8. No later than September 2, 2025:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion.
9. A further case management conference is set for October 21, 2025, at 4:00 p.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.
10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than October 27, 2025. Response to the settlement demand is due November 10, 2025. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on November 17, 2025.
11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, December 4, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Aug-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 19, 2025, in Department 304, Line 4.
No appearances, not contested, The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. 36(a). Opposition filed by Defendant J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc. Reply filed.
Defendant contends that the Declarations of Dr. Sei Lee and Plaintiff's counsel, David Donadio, lack foundation in regard to the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure 36(a). (Opposition, 1-2.) The Court disagrees. Defendant did not provide any contradictory medical opinion, such as a declaration from a licensed physician or a medical doctor, disputing Dr. Lee's opinion. Moreover, Defendant is not a physician qualified to opine on either Plaintiffs' medical records or the validity of Dr. Lee's opinions. As this Court has previously stated, unsupported speculation about a plaintiff's health is not a valid basis to oppose a preference motion grounded in admissible medical evidence. "Where a party meets the requisite standard for calendar preference under [Code of Civil Procedure section 36] subdivision (a), preference must be granted. No weighing of interests is involved." (Fox v. Superior Court (2018) 21 Cal.App.5th 529, 535; see also, e.g., Miller v. Superior Court (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 1200, 1204 [statute "grants a mandatory and absolute right to trial preference"]; Swaithes v. Superior Court (1989) 212 Cal.App.3d 1082, 1085 [trial court "has no power to balance the different interests of opposing litigants in applying the provision"]; Koch-Ash v. Superior Court (1986) 180 Cal.App.3d 689, 694 [section 36(a) "must be deemed to be mandatory and absolute" and "no discretion is left to trial courts."].)
1. The trial date is December 8, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is December 17, 2025.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.
2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 2, 2025.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.
3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.
4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is November 21, 2025.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is December 5, 2025.
7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than September 18, 2025.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

(mini-minutes 1 of 2 continues, see next entry)
